name: Impersonations
description: Become someone else! Impersonate these famous faces, characters, and personalities — no names allowed, just your best impression.

cards.charadesHint: Adopt a perfectly upright posture, wave slowly with a cupped hand, and speak in clipped, precise received pronunciation
Pout, pinch your fingers together, flick your hair dramatically, and speak in short punchy superlatives
Flex your muscles, speak in a thick Austrian accent, and deliver a robotic one-liner with deadpan expression
Slow your speech to a deeply wise, warm drawl and narrate whatever is happening around you as if it's a documentary
Whisper in hushed reverence as you describe a mundane object nearby as if it's an exotic creature in the wild
Groan longingly about food, slap your own forehead, and mutter a slow, dimwitted lament
Speak in a tiny, gravelly voice and rearrange your sentence so the verb comes last, you must
Pull your lips forward, clench your jaw, and speak in an incomprehensible nasally quacking rage
Roll every S into a thick Scottish purr and deliver a suave, unhurried line with total confidence
Greet everyone with exaggerated enthusiasm in a thick Kazakh accent and make an inappropriate cultural observation
Crouch low, stroke an imaginary precious object, hiss softly, and switch between adoration and paranoid suspicion
Grab your jacket collar, hee-hee, spin, and finish with a sharp moonwalk glide and a chin-down pose
Stroke an imaginary cat in your lap, speak in a slow raspy murmur, and make someone an offer they cannot refuse
Sway from side to side, gesture wildly with both hands, and deliver a rambling complaint about rum
Sit with eerie stillness and barely restrained authority, speak in flat emotionless Russian-accented statements
Wrap an invisible cloak around yourself, bare your teeth, and speak in a dramatic Transylvanian drawl
Lean over an imaginary plate with barely contained fury, squint, and deliver a blistering critique in a Scottish-London blend
Slouch, look terrified, point behind you, and speak in a wavering surfer-stoner drawl with your best friend's name
Hold down an imaginary billowing skirt, breathe in a whispery breathy voice, and bat your lashes dramatically
Stand tall with slow mechanical breathing, extend one hand slowly, and deliver a line about being someone's father
Sit sideways, throw up a peace sign, and question an expert earnestly on a topic you clearly don't understand
Sway slightly with a beaming warm smile, wave with a tiny gloved hand, and ask cheerily about nothing in particular
Stop dramatically mid-run, plant your feet wide, throw your head back, and shout your own name triumphantly
Crawl through imaginary undergrowth, sip from a revolting source, and calmly explain why surviving is completely fine
Pat the top of your hair as if adjusting a towering blue beehive and murmur a long-suffering maternal sigh
Straighten your cuffs, introduce yourself surname first then full name, and order a drink with exact specifications
Ruffle your hair frantically, bluster a half-finished classical reference, and look cheerfully unaware of any consequences
Deliver a line in an unhurried, flat East London accent, pausing between each word for maximum understated effect
Open and close your hands like a puppet shark jaw and repeat a catchy tune with increasing family members
Raise one eyebrow as high as possible, fold your arms over an enormous chest, and turn slowly to face your opponent
Sit heavily, gesture with your hands, speak in a thick Jersey drawl, and look offended by a perceived slight
Stand absolutely rigid, pout without smiling, and move only your eyelids while everyone dances around you
Steeple your fingers, stare intensely at a random object, and rattle off a chain of deductions at brilliant speed
Speak in a high, joyful squeak, refer to yourself in third person, and laugh uncontrollably at everything
Squint at someone with relaxed Australian calm and gesture at a comically large imaginary knife
Plant a staff firmly, draw yourself to full height, and declare in thunderous tones that something shall not pass
Strike an extravagant avant-garde pose, perform a line of a pop anthem with total vocal commitment
Lumber around on all fours, whimper at imaginary ghosts, and mangle every word with a dopey canine drawl
Lean in with soft genuine curiosity, ask a surprisingly awkward question in a mild-mannered voice, and nod slowly
Cross your arms, drape yourself in imaginary gold chains, and growl that you pity someone
Pick up an imaginary spoon, look directly at the camera, and describe tasting something with breathless sensual delight
Let your wrists dangle, look slightly anxious and exasperated, and launch into a cheerful speech that descends into panic
Stand with bulldog jowls, flash a V-sign, and deliver a short, defiant speech with rumbling gravitas
Stare blankly, announce a plan to colonise another planet, and laugh slightly too late at your own joke
Adopt a cheery Scottish-nanny demeanour, bustle around tidying, and drop into a gruff male voice accidentally
Shuffle forward with head down, mumble incomprehensibly, look confused, and suddenly burst into a metal shriek
Straighten your tie, declare something minor to be a massive achievement, and pull a rigid television presenter smile
Sway unpredictably, gesture grandly with ringed fingers, and explain a convoluted plan involving rum and ships
Crouch low with breathless Australian excitement, reach gently for an invisible dangerous animal, and exclaim its beauty
Strike a provocative pose with pointed collar, deliver a verse in a mid-Atlantic accent, and reinvent yourself mid-song
Fawn obsequiously over one guest, then turn and silently scream in barely suppressed fury at absolutely everything
Stand powerfully, belt a note at full force, snap a hand to a hip, and command the room like a stadium
Lean back laughing at yourself, then launch into an increasingly escalating story about something embarrassing happening
Bat your eyelashes with theatrical vanity, flick imaginary hair, and karate chop the air when disrespected
Strum an invisible acoustic guitar with laid-back cool and deliver a raspy sun-soaked vocal about simple pleasures
Adopt a completely different character mid-scene with a new accent, remain utterly committed, and ask a deeply uncomfortable question
Stand very still in an imaginary duffel coat, produce a marmalade sandwich from your hat, and give a hard stare
Open your arms to an invisible stadium crowd, tear up slightly, and sing a big triumphant chorus with full swagger
Lean into the mic, deliver a rapid grime verse with precise flow, then switch to a soulful hook with arms wide
Sit upright with serene dignity, speak in a crisp clipped accent about duty and service, and smile warmly but with total reserve
